prompt: A vintage 1960s French New Wave cinema poster collage on aged beige paper, designed like a torn-paper art-house one-sheet with analog grain, distressed print texture, rough ripped edges, ink wear, halftone imperfections, and slight misregistration. Center the composition around a large black-and-white portrait of a woman with messy dark hair, hand raised to her face, holding a cigarette, cropped from the chest up, with her face obscured by a rectangular blur block. Use a layered collage layout with exactly 8 major paper elements: 1) a top-left black text block reading "LES FILMS DU CARREFOUR présentent" above the large stacked name "ANNA KARINA" and smaller text "dans un film de JEAN-LUC GODARD"; 2) a red torn quote panel on the left reading "Il faut essayer d’être jeune comme on peut. - GODARD"; 3) a blue torn panel in the upper right with a black label reading "PRIX JEAN VIGO 1962"; 4) a narrow torn newspaper clipping on the upper right reading "l’amour est plus fort que la mort"; 5) a yellow torn panel on the mid-right showing a black silhouette of a man in a hat facing a woman, beside a vertical black filmstrip border with white sprocket holes; 6) a bottom-left black-and-white Paris street photo with wet pavement, a lamppost, pedestrians, and a visible sign reading "HOTEL DU NORD"; 7) a small bottom-left review text block reading '"LE FILM QUI A CHANGÉ LE CINÉMA" - CAHIERS DU CINÉMA'; 8) a bottom-right cropped black-and-white portrait of a man in a suit with his face obscured by a rectangular blur block, partially covered by a red torn paper shape. Across the lower middle, place the huge torn-paper title in bold condensed uppercase: "À BOUT DE" in black and "SOUFFLE" in red, slightly angled and spanning most of the width. Under the title, add a blue torn strip with white uppercase text "UNE HISTOIRE D’AMOUR ET DE LIBERTÉ". At the bottom center, add "COMING SOON" in black uppercase and below it "LES FILMS DU CARREFOUR". Limit the palette to faded cream, black, deep ultramarine blue, muted poster red, and mustard yellow. The overall mood is rebellious, romantic, and cinematic, like an iconic French New Wave poster made from ripped magazines, street photography, and bold typography.
